1	// Package bead implements the on-disk bead tracker.
2	//
3	// Size envelope (ddx-f8a11202):
4	//
5	// Individual bead fields — description, acceptance, notes, and each event's
6	// body — are bounded by MaxFieldBytes (65,535 bytes). This matches upstream
7	// bd's Dolt TEXT column limit so DDx-authored beads can always round-trip
8	// through `bd import`. Writers exceeding the cap are truncated with a
9	// `…[truncated, N bytes]` marker at AppendEvent; callers that need to
10	// preserve the full payload (notably reviewer streams) should persist to a
11	// sidecar artifact under `.ddx/executions/<id>/` and record a path
12	// reference in the event body.
13	//
14	// On the read side, scanners use a 16MB buffer — real-world incidents have
15	// produced 1.46MB bead rows and ~7MB session-log rows when writers bypassed
16	// the cap. 16MB comfortably fits a bead with dozens of maxed-out fields and
17	// matches the scanner in the agent package.
18	package bead
19	
20	import (
21		"bufio"
22		"bytes"
23		"context"
24		"crypto/rand"
25		"encoding/hex"
26		"encoding/json"
27		"fmt"
28		"os"
29		"path/filepath"
30		"sort"
31		"strconv"
32		"strings"
33		"time"
34	
35		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
36		gitpkg "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/git"
37	)
38	
39	// Store manages beads via a pluggable backend.
40	type Store struct {
41		Collection string
42		Dir        string
43		File       string
44		Prefix     string
45		LockDir    string
46		LockWait   time.Duration
47		backend    RawBackend // nil means use built-in JSONL
48	}
49	
50	// Compile-time check: *Store satisfies the high-level Backend interface
51	// declared in backend.go. This is the contract chaos_test.go and (eventually)
52	// cli/cmd callers program against.
53	var _ Backend = (*Store)(nil)
54	
55	type StoreOption func(*Store)
56	
57	// WithCollection selects the logical bead collection. The default collection
58	// remains "beads", which maps to "beads.jsonl" in the JSONL backend.
59	func WithCollection(name string) StoreOption {
60		return func(s *Store) {
61			if cleaned := normalizeCollection(name); cleaned != "" {
62				s.Collection = cleaned
63			}
64		}
65	}
66	
67	// NewStore creates a store with the given directory.
68	// Defaults can be overridden via options or environment.
69	func NewStore(dir string, opts ...StoreOption) *Store {
70		if dir == "" {
71			dir = envOr("DDX_BEAD_DIR", ".ddx")
72		}
73		workingDir := dir
74		if filepath.Base(dir) == ".ddx" {
75			workingDir = filepath.Dir(dir)
76		}
77		prefix := envOr("DDX_BEAD_PREFIX", "")
78		var configBackend string
79		if cfg, err := config.LoadWithWorkingDir(workingDir); err == nil && cfg != nil && cfg.Bead != nil {
80			if prefix == "" && cfg.Bead.IDPrefix != "" {
81				prefix = cfg.Bead.IDPrefix
82			}
83			configBackend = cfg.Bead.Backend
84		}
85		if prefix == "" {
86			prefix = detectPrefix(workingDir)
87		}
88		// Backend selection: env var wins, then config, then jsonl default.
89		backendType := os.Getenv("DDX_BEAD_BACKEND")
90		if backendType == "" {
91			backendType = configBackend
92		}
93		if backendType == "" {
94			backendType = BackendJSONL
95		}
96	
97		s := &Store{
98			Collection: DefaultCollection,
99			Dir:        dir,
100			Prefix:     prefix,

642	func TestClaimMetadataKeys_SharedByReopenAndUnclaim(t *testing.T) {
643		s := newTestStore(t)
644		b := &Bead{ID: "ddx-ckmeta-001", Title: "Claim metadata key test", IssueType: "task", Status: StatusOpen}
645		require.NoError(t, s.Create(testCtx(), b))
646	
647		// Helper: populate all ClaimMetadataExtraKeys plus heartbeat and a survivor key.
648		populateClaimMeta := func(id string) {
649			require.NoError(t, s.Update(testCtx(), id, func(b *Bead) {
650				if b.Extra == nil {
651					b.Extra = make(map[string]any)
652				}
653				for _, k := range ClaimMetadataExtraKeys {
654					b.Extra[k] = "test-value"
655				}
656				b.Extra[ClaimHeartbeatExtraKey] = "test-heartbeat"
657				b.Extra["claim-test-extra"] = "should-survive"
658			}))
659		}
660	
661		// --- AC4.a + AC4.b: Unclaim clears all ClaimMetadataExtraKeys ---
662		require.NoError(t, s.Claim(b.ID, "worker"))
663		populateClaimMeta(b.ID)
664		require.NoError(t, s.Unclaim(b.ID))
665	
666		got, err := s.Get(testCtx(), b.ID)
667		require.NoError(t, err)
668		for _, k := range ClaimMetadataExtraKeys {
669			assert.NotContains(t, got.Extra, k, "Unclaim must clear %q", k)
670		}
671		// AC4.d: unrelated key survives Unclaim
672		assert.Equal(t, "should-survive", got.Extra["claim-test-extra"], "Unclaim must not clear unrelated Extra keys")
673	
674		// --- AC4.a + AC4.c: Reopen clears all ClaimMetadataExtraKeys ---
675		// Close the bead so Reopen has something to reopen.
676		require.NoError(t, s.Claim(b.ID, "worker"))
677		populateClaimMeta(b.ID)
678		require.NoError(t, s.Close(testCtx(), b.ID))
679	
680		require.NoError(t, s.Reopen(b.ID, "test reopen", ""))
681	
682		got, err = s.Get(testCtx(), b.ID)
683		require.NoError(t, err)
684		for _, k := range ClaimMetadataExtraKeys {
685			assert.NotContains(t, got.Extra, k, "Reopen must clear %q", k)
686		}
687		// AC4.d: unrelated key survives Reopen
688		assert.Equal(t, "should-survive", got.Extra["claim-test-extra"], "Reopen must not clear unrelated Extra keys")
689	}
690	
691	// withHeartbeat temporarily overrides HeartbeatInterval and HeartbeatTTL for
692	// the duration of a test.
693	func withHeartbeat(t *testing.T, interval, ttl time.Duration) {
694		t.Helper()
695		origInterval := HeartbeatInterval
696		origTTL := HeartbeatTTL
697		HeartbeatInterval = interval
698		HeartbeatTTL = ttl
699		t.Cleanup(func() {
700			HeartbeatInterval = origInterval
701			HeartbeatTTL = origTTL
702		})
703	}
704	
705	func TestHeartbeatReclaimStaleInProgressBead(t *testing.T) {
706		withHeartbeat(t, 10*time.Millisecond, 50*time.Millisecond)
707	
708		s := newTestStore(t)
709		b := &Bead{ID: "ddx-hb-stale", Title: "Stale claim"}
710		require.NoError(t, s.Create(testCtx(), b))
711	
712		// First worker claims it normally.
713		require.NoError(t, s.Claim(b.ID, "worker-a"))
714	
715		// Forge a stale runtime lease and a stale legacy tracker heartbeat.
716		stale := time.Now().UTC().Add(-1 * time.Hour)
717		staleRec := ClaimLeaseRecord{
718			BeadID:    b.ID,
719			UpdatedAt: stale,
720			PID:       os.Getpid(),
721		}
722		data, err := json.Marshal(staleRec)
723		require.NoError(t, err)
724		require.NoError(t, writeAtomicClaimFile(claimLivenessPath(s.Dir, b.ID), append(data, '\n')))
725		require.NoError(t, s.Update(testCtx(), b.ID, func(bd *Bead) {
726			if bd.Extra == nil {
727				bd.Extra = map[string]any{}
728			}
729			staleStr := stale.Format(time.RFC3339)
730			bd.Extra["work-heartbeat-at"] = staleStr
731			bd.Extra["claimed-at"] = staleStr
732		}))
733	
734		// A fresh worker must be able to reclaim the stalled bead.
735		require.NoError(t, s.Claim(b.ID, "worker-b"))
736		got, err := s.Get(testCtx(), b.ID)
737		require.NoError(t, err)
738		assert.Equal(t, StatusInProgress, got.Status)
739		assert.Equal(t, "worker-b", got.Owner)
740	
741		// The ready-execution queue must surface the stale bead too.
742		s2 := newTestStore(t)
743		orig := &Bead{ID: "ddx-hb-stale-2", Title: "Stale claim 2"}
744		require.NoError(t, s2.Create(testCtx(), orig))
745		require.NoError(t, s2.Claim(orig.ID, "worker-a"))
746		staleLease := ClaimLeaseRecord{
747			BeadID:    orig.ID,
748			UpdatedAt: time.Now().UTC().Add(-1 * time.Hour),
749			PID:       os.Getpid(),
750		}
751		data, err = json.Marshal(staleLease)
752		require.NoError(t, err)
753		require.NoError(t, writeAtomicClaimFile(claimLivenessPath(s2.Dir, orig.ID), append(data, '\n')))
754		ready, err := s2.ReadyExecution()
755		require.NoError(t, err)
756		require.Len(t, ready, 1)
757		assert.Equal(t, orig.ID, ready[0].ID)
758	}
759	
760	func TestReady_StaleClaimedInProgressIsReady(t *testing.T) {
761		withHeartbeat(t, 10*time.Millisecond, 50*time.Millisecond)

data.text:
Perfect! The refactoring has already been completed successfully. Let me verify all acceptance criteria are met:

**Acceptance Criteria Verification:**

1. ✅ **clearClaimExtraKeys helper exists** - Created at lines 1051-1061 in store.go
2. ✅ **Reopen uses clearClaimExtraKeys** - Line 1671 calls `clearClaimExtraKeys(b.Extra)` instead of inline loop
3. ✅ **Unclaim uses clearClaimExtraKeys** - Line 1076 calls `clearClaimExtraKeys(b.Extra)` instead of inline loop  
4. ✅ **Both methods call RemoveClaimHeartbeat** - Unclaim at line 1081, Reopen at line 1746
5. ✅ **Tests pass** - TestClaimMetadataKeys_SharedByReopenAndUnclaim and all claim tests pass
6. ✅ **Full bead package tests pass** - All 42+ tests in ./internal/bead pass

The commit `776946cc6` shows the exact changes:
- Created `clearClaimExtraKeys` helper that consolidates the parallel loops
- Replaced 9 lines of inline loop + nil check in Unclaim with a single helper call
- Replaced 4 lines of inline loop in Reopen with a single helper call

The refactoring eliminates duplication and makes future updates to `ClaimMetadataExtraKeys` automatically apply to both methods. The work is complete and verified.
